Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Class Notice;
Off Calendar Apr-09-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROSS L. LIBENSON OF THE HOFFMAN, LIBENSON SAUNDERS & BARBA, TEL. NO. (510) 763-5700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PIYA MUKHERJEE OF THE LAW FIRM OF BLUMENTHAL, NORDREHAUG & BHOWMIK, TEL. NO. (858) 551-1223 EXT. 3 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ANDREW R. LIVINGSTON, TEL. NO. 773-5588 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT UNION BANK;

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING NOTICE AND VERIFICATION OF EMPLOYMENT. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES Motion For Approval Of Class Notice is denied with 30 days leave to amend. While the burdens of discovery are rarely imposed upon absent Plaintiff class members, (Phillips Petroleum Co. v. Shutts (1985) 472 U.S. 797, 810), CRC Rule 3.768 explicitly permits such discovery. Thus, although unlikely, discovery is possible and the language indicating that no action need be taken as a participant should be excluded. However, Defendant's proposed addition of language indicating that such discovery may be required is similarly not useful to the purpose of this notice and need not be added. In addition, in balancing the interests of adequate time for trial preparation with the importance of providing adequate time for class members to review and consider whether to opt out of the class, the notice period is set at 60 days. Moreover, requiring opt out requests to be mailed is itself not unduly burdensome, but here the notice seems to require specific language in that mailing. Instead, a less burdensome opt-out provision should be used, stating that: "The request should state that you want to be excluded from Mathies v. Union Bank."
The notice should be corrected with respect to the courthouse hours to reflect the correct hours (8:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.). Furthermore, while neither the pre-approval of an outer envelope nor a plan for re-mailing returned notices is required, both increase the chance of notifying Plaintiff-class members. However, requiring these steps at this stage of the proceedings would be unfairly prejudicial to Plaintiffs given Defendant's failure to raise either issue in the six months since it received the notice for review, and are therefore not required. Finally, Defendant's request for an order prohibiting class counsel from communicating with class members during the notice period is denied. Defendant points to no authority prohibiting such communications by class counsel. DEFENDANT TO PAY FOR COST OF POST-CARDS. ORDER SIGNED with exhibit 1 ATTACHED as the REVISED CLASS NOTICE. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
